title: Bridgeworld Lost

|release date=After [[104 ABY]]<ref name="Date">"[[Celestia Galactica Photografica]]" was included in ''[[Star Wars Legends Epic Collection: Legacy Vol. 4]]'', which describes the stories it contains as taking place more than a [[Standard century/Legends|century]] after the events of [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']]. That film took place in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]] according to ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'', meaning that all [[Painting/Legends|paintings]] in "Celestia Galactica Photografica," including ''Bridgeworld Lost'', must have been created after [[104 ABY]].</ref>
|media type=[[Painting/Legends|Painting]]<ref name="CGP">{{StoryCite|story=Celestia Galactica Photografica|book=Star Wars: Visionaries}}</ref>

'''''Bridgeworld Lost''''' was a [[Painting/Legends|painting]]<ref name="CGP" /> created after [[104 ABY]]<ref name="Date" /> set during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]<ref name="CGP" /> between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]], which lasted from [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]] to [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]].<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> It depicted a group of [[Skakoan/Legends|Skakoans]]<ref name="CGP" />—a [[Species/Legends|species]] associated with the [[Techno Union/Legends|Techno Union]], a member organization of the Confederacy<ref name="Alien">''[[The New Essential Guide to Alien Species]]''</ref>—surrounded by [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]]-wielding [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] of the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]]. The background of the painting contained [[Bridge city/Legends|bridge cities]] and three [[Munificent-class star frigate/Legends|''Munificent''-class star frigates]].<ref name="CGP" />

==Behind the scenes==
''Bridgeworld Lost'' appeared in "[[Celestia Galactica Photografica]]," a collection of in-universe illustrations by [[Ryan Church]] collected in ''[[Star Wars: Visionaries]]'',<ref name="CGP" /> a graphic novel edited by [[Jeremy Barlow]] and released on [[April 2]], [[2005]].<ref name="Inside Star Wars: Visionaries">{{SWArchive|url=eu/lit/comics/news20050315.html|text=Inside ''Star Wars: Visionaries''}}</ref> The concept of a "bridge world" was previously developed<ref name="Cato Neimoidia">{{DB|location|catoneimoidia|Cato Neimoidia}}</ref> by Church in [[2002]]<ref name="Concept">{{WebCite|url=http://ryanchurch.com/star-wars-iii|text=Star Wars III|author=[[Ryan Church|Church, Ryan]]|archivedate=20220321091837}}</ref> during production of the [[prequel trilogy]] film [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']],<ref name="Concept" /> which premiered on [[May 19]], [[2005]].<ref name="Year">''[[Star Wars Year By Year: A Visual History, Updated and Expanded Edition]]''</ref> It was intended to be the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Saleucami/Legends|Saleucami]] and to serve as the home of the Techno Union. The designs were instead used for the planet [[Cato Neimoidia/Legends|Cato Neimoidia]] in the finished film.<ref name="Cato Neimoidia" /> Church additionally created a concept art piece titled "[[:File:Mygeeto_Surrender.jpg|Mygeeto Surrender]]" on [[December 6]], 2002. The image featured clone troopers holding up [[Muun/Legends|Muun]] members of the [[InterGalactic Banking Clan/Legends|InterGalactic Banking Clan]] on<ref name="Insider 86">{{InsiderCite|84|The Art of Revenge of the Sith|The Art of ''Revenge of the Sith'', Part Two}}</ref> the planet<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> [[Mygeeto/Legends|Mygeeto]].<ref name="Insider 86" />
